name: mouse_major_satellite
description: Mouse major satellite (MaSat): 234 bp AT-rich monomer in vast tandem arrays in pericentromeric heterochromatin of all autosomes and the X. Arrays span several megabases per chromosome and account for ~3-5% of the mouse genome. Transcribed into non-coding RNAs that participate in heterochromatin maintenance. Lacks the CENP-B box, distinguishing it from minor satellite.
